title: Abetting mutiny, or attempting to seduce a soldier, sailor or airman from his duty
chapter_title: Offences Relating to the Army, Navy & Air Force
content: Whoever abets the committing of mutiny by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman, in the Army, Navy or Air Force, or attempts to seduce any such person from his allegiance or his duty, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

footnotes.text: The act of 'seducing' involves corrupting the loyalty of service members to the state, turning them against their constitutional duties or encouraging revolt.

title: Abetment of assault by soldier, sailor or airman on his superior officer, when in execution of his office
chapter_title: Offences Relating to the Army, Navy & Air Force
content: Whoever abets an assault by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman, in the Army, Navy or Air Force, on any superior officer being in the execution of his office, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine.

footnotes.text: This protects the chain of command. Even if the assault does not physically occur, the *abetment* (incitement) itself is a serious criminal offense.
